source: The Market (themarket.ch) — The one thing that matters is inflation
summary: Semiconductors represent a uniquely transformative technology with secular demand growth driven by AI
summary_extended: Baker characterizes semiconductors as 'the closest thing to magic in the modern world,' expressing high structural conviction in the sector's long-term demand trajectory. He argues that secular demand growth in semiconductors is being driven by the accelerating adoption of AI across industries, which he views as a fundamental and durable tailwind rather than a cyclical phenomenon. The investment implication is a broad long-term bullish stance on the semiconductor sector, though Baker does not specify individual companies or sub-segments in this particular thesis excerpt. No specific numbers, price targets, or timeframes are cited beyond the general framing of long-term secular growth tied to AI adoption.
detail: Baker characterizes semiconductors as 'the closest thing to magic in the modern world,' signaling high structural conviction in the sector's long-term demand trajectory, particularly as AI adoption accelerates across industries.
confidence_evidence: Superlative framing ('closest thing to magic') and explicit anticipation of a surge in demand due to AI rise, consistent with Baker's known long-term semiconductor positioning.
quote_fragment: semiconductors are the closest thing to magic in the modern world
